name: Detect Ambush
description: Training which develops an eye for spotting location and terrain suitable for ambushes and being ambushed. It also provides a rudimentary knowledge of guerrilla tactics used by the enemy.

name: Detect Concealment
description: This is a skill which enables the individual to spot and recognize camouflage, concealed structures/building and vehicles, as well as the ability to construct unobtrusive shelters, use camouflage and blend into the environment.

name: Disguise
description: The character knows how to apply make-up, wigs, skin putty, dies, and other special effects in order to alter his appearance or that of somebody else.

name: Escape Artist
description: The methods, principles, and tricks of escape artists. Includes muscle control (tensing and relaxing muscles), flexing and popping joints, knowledge of knots, and the ability to conceal small objects on the person. The character can try slipping out of handcuffs, ropes, straitjacket, etc. Note: Picking locks is a separate and distinct skill.

name: Fieldcraft
description: This skill covers the common "tricks" spies use to exchange information covertly.

narrativeAdvantages.summary: Covers dead drops: information left in a hiding place (e.g. taped under a park bench or behind a brick) for another agent to retrieve without ever meeting the contact.
narrativeAdvantages.appliesWhen: When exchanging information covertly via dead drop.

narrativeAdvantages.summary: Can select and use code words and passwords to recognize other agents.
narrativeAdvantages.appliesWhen: When identifying fellow agents or handlers.

interdependentKnowledge.summary: Noticing and avoiding being shadowed uses this skill in conjunction with the Perception rules (Nightbane RPG, page 66).

name: Forgery
description: The techniques of making false copies of official documents, signatures, passports, I.D.s, and other printed material. The forger must have an original or photocopy to work from in order to make an accurate copy.

name: Imitate Voices & Impersonation

splitBaseTracks.label: Specific person's voice, inflection, and attitude

description: The ability to imitate the voice, accent, and expressions of another person or regional dialect. Common among thieves, assassins, and other villains.

subTasks.name: Regional disguise of an average local person (farmer, laborer, vagabond, etc.)
Impersonate a person in a specific occupation (sheriff, guard, inspector, messenger, merchant, priest, etc.)

interdependentKnowledge.summary: Full impersonation sub-tasks require successful Disguise and Intelligence rolls in addition to this skill. Convincing impersonation typically includes knowledge of the subject, military procedure, dress, heraldry/rank, and fluent use of the relevant language.

sequentialChecks.summary: Disguise must succeed before attempting an impersonation sub-task.
sequentialChecks.condition: When attempting an impersonation sub-task (average local, occupation, or known person).

sequentialChecks.summary: Intelligence must succeed before attempting an impersonation sub-task.
sequentialChecks.condition: When attempting an impersonation sub-task (average local, occupation, or known person).

situationalRuleOverrides.summary: Specific-person voice mimicry requires knowing the person very well or spending hours studying them.
The longer the character remains under others' scrutiny while impersonating, the more likely something reveals the impostor.

yields.summary: Disguised normal/true speaking voice; convincingly sounds from another region or part of the world.
Accurately imitates the target's voice, inflections, and attitude.
With successful Disguise and Intelligence rolls, convincingly impersonates the chosen subject using accurate disguise, proper action, and fluent language.

failureResults.summary: Voice mimicry failure: the regional accent or specific-person imitation is unconvincing. Impersonation failure (with Disguise and Intelligence): some element of the impersonation is flawed—most likely incorrect behavior or a character trait, or lack of information about the person being impersonated ("Tell me again what you said that night," etc.). Such holes give the character away sooner or later.

name: Intelligence
description: This is the specific training in the practices and principles of recognizing and analyzing sources of information about the enemy, observation techniques, counterintelligence measures and proper procedure. This includes the practical assessment of sights and sounds, estimation of ranges, what to report, handling prisoners of war, and handling captured documents and equipment (tagging and reporting to group leaders or proper authorities). This means the character will be able to accurately estimate ranges, the number of enemies, direction, purpose, and assess the importance of specific information. Further intelligence training includes a working knowledge of indigenous guerrilla warfare, enemy practices, appearance, and current activities. This enables the intelligence officer to recognize suspicious activity as guerrilla actions and identify guerrilla operatives. For example: A particular booby trap, or weapon or mode of operation may be indicative of guerrilla activity in the area. It may be up to the character to confirm the existence of the enemy and their strengths, number, and location. Another area of training made available to intelligence is the identification of enemy troops, officers, and foreign advisors. This means the person learns the many distinguishing ranks and marks that identify specific military units, special forces, groups, and leaders of the enemy. Such identification can pinpoint and confirm enemy operations, goals, and movement, as well as confirm outside intervention/aid.
failureResults: A failed roll in any of the areas of intelligence means that evidence is inconclusive, or that the character has incorrectly assessed the information/situation and is uncertain. A failed roll involving individual clues may mean the person has dismissed it entirely as being meaningless (game masters use your discretion).

name: Interrogation Techniques
description: This skill is learned by policemen, intelligence officers, etc. The character knows the techniques to get information from (typically unwilling) subjects. This includes such old methods as "good cop/bad cop" (one interrogator is threatening and intimidating, the other is sympathetic and friendly), deceiving and misleading the subject into giving away information, and similar methods. This skill also includes some basic knowledge on methods of torture, from basic tactics like depriving the subject of sleep, to the old "medieval" instruments and new, sophisticated techniques like sensory deprivation, partial electrocution, and drugs. Note: Only evil characters will engage in torture routinely.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: The character can judge if the subject is lying; the game master may assess bonuses and penalties depending on how good a liar the subject is.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: When using interrogation to detect deception.

name: Pick Locks
description: The character knows the methods and tools for picking/opening key and basic tumbler type locks. This does not include sophisticated computer or electronic locks.

performanceFormulas.linkSummary: Each attempt to pick a lock takes 1D6 melee rounds.

name: Pick Pockets
description: An ability to remove items from a person without their being aware of it.

failureResults.summary: If a pick pocket attempt fails, the item has NOT been removed.

secondaryCheck.appliesWhen: After a failed pick pocket attempt.
secondaryCheck.subject: intended_victim
secondaryCheck.resolutionNote: 67% likelihood of the intended victim recognizing the intent of the action.
secondaryCheck.ifTriggers: The victim recognizes the intent of the action.
secondaryCheck.ifNotTriggers: The victim does not recognize the intent.

name: Prowl
description: This skill helps the character to move with stealth; quietly, slowly, and carefully. Techniques include balance and footing, short steps and pacing, rifle positioning, prone positions for low visibility, and crawling.
failureResults: The character has been seen or heard.
successOutcomes: The character is not seen or heard and may make a sneak attack.

conditionalRelatedSkills.summary: +5% bonus to Tailing when the character also has Prowl.

name: Sniper
description: This skill represents special training in long-range rifle firing and marksmanship.

situationalRuleOverrides.summary: Only rifles that can be made to fire a single round or blast can be used for sniping (no automatic/multi-firing rifles).

name: Tracking

situationalModifiers.task: Follow the trail of this character when they are deliberately using Counter-Tracking
situationalModifiers.condition: When the character is deliberately trying to conceal their trail using Counter-Tracking techniques. The character's rate of travel is reduced by half while doing so.

description: Visual tracking is the identification of tracks, and following the path of men or animals by the signs they leave on the ground and vegetation. Tracking is a precise art, requiring much practice. The skill includes the evaluation of tracks, indicating whether the person being tracked is loaded down with equipment, running, moving slowly (by measuring the space between steps), and so on. By this means, the tracker can estimate the person's rate of movement, apparent direction, the number of persons in the party, and whether the person knows he is being followed. Other methods of tracking require recognizing other telltale signs, such as blood and other stains, broken and displaced vegetation, overturned rocks, litter (such as cigarette butts, ration cans, candy wrappers, soiled bandages and camp fire remains), and even odors carried by the wind. Tracking also includes the recognizing of dangerous animals by their tracks, habits and feces. Likewise, tire tracks of vehicles can reveal much, such as size and type of the vehicle, the weight of its load, etc. Counter-Tracking techniques are also known, such as covering one's trail, misdirection, parallel trails, avoiding obvious pitfalls like littering and others.
failureResults: A failed roll means that the signs are inconclusive, vague or misleading. Additional signs must be found to verify or clarify (roll again).

resolutionLogic.summary: Three consecutive failed rolls means the tracker has completely lost the trail.

recurrence.summary: Roll once every 40 yards/meters when following a trail.

name: Undercover Ops

prerequisites.narrativeRequirement: Typically reserved for law enforcement, espionage agents, military Special Forces, mercenaries, con artists, and other criminal types.

description: Training in undercover operations in which the character learns the methods and techniques of blending smoothly into the background and appearing as if he belongs there (just another unmemorable face in the crowd or one of the guys), as well as assuming a false identity and playing a role to track, spy upon, or gather information or evidence. Also includes methods for the covert exchange of information necessary for keeping in touch with the individual's handler, such as dead drops, ways to avoid or notice someone tailing them, selecting passwords or phrases, and similar techniques.

successOutcomes.summary: Blends into the background convincingly, maintains cover while playing a role, and executes covert handler contact without exposing the operation.

failureResults.summary: Cover is compromised, the false identity rings false under scrutiny, or covert exchanges risk exposure.

interdependentKnowledge.summary: Ways to avoid or notice someone tailing the character overlap with Tailing skill knowledge.
False identities and cover roles are often supported by Disguise and related tradecraft.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Covert handler contact may use dead drops (leaving information in a hiding place for another agent or courier), passwords or phrases, and similar tradecraft.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: When maintaining contact with a handler or organization while undercover.

name: Wilderness Survival
description: Techniques for getting water, food, shelter, and help when stranded in wild forests, deserts, or mountains.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Characters without this skill will not be able to stay healthy for more than a few days in the wilderness once their supplies run out.
